studio@anavai.com

The Ritual of Making

From conversation
to installation

Every Anavai wall begins as a dialogue. We believe the process of making is as important as the result — and we share it openly with every client.

01Consultation
02Design & Sample
03Hand-Painting
04Installation

Total lead time from first conversation to installation: 6–10 weeks depending on complexity and scale.

Begin a Project

01

Consultation &
Space Reading

Before any pattern is discussed, we want to understand your space — its light, its proportions, its relationship to nature, and the energy you wish to cultivate within it. We do this through a detailed questionnaire followed by a video or in-person conversation with one of our founders.

We ask about the people who will inhabit the space, the function of each room, and what kind of transformation the wall should perform. Only then do we propose an element and a collection.

1–2 hrsConsultation time
FreeNo commitment

02

Design Development
& Sample Panels

Based on the consultation, our artists develop a pattern proposal — beginning with sketches and colour studies, moving to a hand-painted sample panel (30×30cm or 50×50cm) that we ship to you.

The sample lets you experience the real texture, light-response, and presence of the work in your actual space before any production begins. We refine through up to three rounds of feedback at no additional cost.

7–14 daysSample delivery
3Revision rounds
IncludedNo extra cost

03

The Art of
Hand-Painting

Once a sample is approved, we begin production. Each roll is painted by hand in our studio using water-based, eco-certified pigments on FSC-certified substrate. There is no mechanical reproduction — every mark is made by a human hand.

This means that no two panels are identical. The slight variations in stroke, texture, and pigment depth are what makes each installation alive — and what distinguishes Anavai from any industrially produced wallpaper.

4–6 weeksProduction time
HandEvery mark
UniqueEvery panel

04

Installation &
Handover

We partner with specialist wallpaper installers across Europe who understand the fragility and precision required for hand-painted work. For significant commissions, one of our founders attends the installation to ensure the panels are placed with correct attention to light source, pattern alignment, and room proportion.

After installation we provide a care guide, leftover panel storage (offcuts are retained in our studio), and a certificate of authenticity for each commission.

IncludedCare guide
Cert.Of authenticity
EuropeInstallation network

Circular Luxury Craft

How we make
responsibly

Zero Overproduction

We produce nothing without a confirmed order. No stock sits in a warehouse. Every panel is made for a specific wall in a specific room — zero waste by design.

FSC-Certified Materials

All paper and substrate carries FSC certification. Our pigments are water-based and free from heavy metals. No solvents. No VOC off-gassing.

Local Artisan Network

We source materials locally and work with artisans within 200km of our studio. Short supply chains mean lower transport emissions and stronger communities.

Offcut Upcycling

Every scrap of hand-painted paper is transformed — into small artworks, custom packaging, or raw material for collaborators. Nothing is thrown away.

Longevity Over Trend

Art-driven work doesn't date. Our patterns are not seasonal. A well-chosen Anavai wall is designed to be relevant — and beautiful — for decades.

Transparent Pricing

We publish our price range openly. No hidden costs, no inflated markups for distributors. What you pay goes directly to artists and materials.

Anavai

Artisanal wallpaper for spaces that hold meaning.
